topics: Andrej Karpathy joining Anthropic and recursive self-improvement
AI industry PR crisis and public backlash
AI regulation and US-China tech competition
Corporate AI-driven layoffs and workforce surveillance

claims.claim: Anthropic was cash-flow positive for the Wall Street Journal in the most recent quarter, signaling strong ROI for the AI sector
evidence: Anthropic described as having reached a positive financial position in the most recent quarter
OpenAI and Anthropic combined estimated at ~$100 billion ARR with ~80% gross margins on inference
All LLM providers combined projected to reach $200-400 billion ARR by end of year

claims.claim: Andrej Karpathy joining Anthropic to lead a pre-training team focused on recursive self-improvement could accelerate model quality improvements by an order of magnitude per year
evidence: Karpathy coined 'vibe coding' and built Auto Research, an open-source training tool with 82,000+ GitHub stars
Gavin Baker argues Karpathy has been 'at the wave upon wave of AI' from Tesla FSD to OpenAI co-founding
Chamath's statistic of 10x-ing every year described as potentially 'conservative' if recursive self-improvement is unlocked

claims.claim: Public backlash against AI is driven by perceived power asymmetry, foreign state interference, and an anti-humanist psychological reaction
evidence: Three commencement speeches booed, including Eric Schmidt's
Freeberg attributes backlash to economic asymmetry (few profiting while many are uncertain), possible CCP-funded campaigns, and AI being 'anti-humanist' like the Copernican Revolution
Gavin Baker suggests a 'CCP funded campaign against AI and data centers in America'

claims.claim: A Trump administration executive order on AI frontier models was drafted and then pulled, reportedly because the president objected to provisions requiring federal government oversight of frontier AI models
evidence: Leaked attendee list included major Neo Lab CEOs and hyperscaler CEOs including Nikesh Arora of Palo Alto Networks
Chamath states the EO 'was scrubbed an hour ago' with the president objecting to aspects requiring 'supervision, insight, review from the federal government' of frontier models

claims.claim: AI-driven layoffs at companies like Cloudflare and Meta are now occurring not because of overhiring but because AI is replacing measurement, management, and knowledge-worker roles
evidence: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince announced 20%+ workforce reduction despite 'record revenue growth' and 'strong free cashflow', citing elimination of 'measurers'
Meta laid off ~8,000 employees concurrently with deploying recording software on employee computers to train AI models
Zuckerberg stated internal engineers are used to teach coding models because internal talent quality exceeds contractors

claims.claim: Google covertly installed a 4-gigabyte Gemini Nano model in Chrome without notifying users, triggering privacy concerns
evidence: Described as happening 'about two weeks ago, very quietly'
Model performs proofreading, spelling, and autocomplete tasks locally
Chamath pushed back on the characterization of Google as a 'bad actor', arguing focus should be on user utility

claims.claim: Dario Amodei's public warnings about AI risk are a rational business strategy to create a regulatory moat that benefits Anthropic as an incumbent
evidence: Chamath argues 'breathlessness has ramped up' correlating with successive fundraising rounds
Gavin Baker states Dario is 'trading his own book' and creating 'boundary conditions for a regulatory moat because he will be inside of the tent pissing out'
Gavin says a 'Nash agent' would independently derive this strategy

quote_candidates.text: if OpenAI and Anthropic are at, call it a hundred billion dollars of ARR now with 80% ish gross margins on inference, like the returns are there
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

quote_candidates.text: I think Chamath's statistics of, you know, 10x-ing every year, you know, might seem conservative if that comes to pass
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

quote_candidates.text: Stop breathlessly asking these model makers what they think. Go to the end user and ask the person in the factory that's using the model and ask him what he or she thinks.
quote_candidates.speaker: Chamath Palihapitiya

quote_candidates.text: I think there is like an underlying view that technology creates leverage for a small group of people, which creates power imbalances and nothing represents that more than AI
quote_candidates.speaker: David Freeberg

quote_candidates.text: I do think it's incumbent on all of us as Americans who are involved in the technology industry in one way or another, to be advocates for the positive, optimistic possibilities that AI introduces to everyone in this world. Because it is starting to feel or seem like there may be a CCP funded campaign against AI and data centers in America.
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

quote_candidates.text: He said he didn't accept that as an answer. He found he did an enormous amount of research with LLM's and found an existing safe drug on the market that they thought would have a meaningful impact on his daughter's condition.
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

guest_candidates.name: Andrej Karpathy
guest_candidates.title: Head of Pre-training, Anthropic (incoming); previously Co-founder, OpenAI; Director of AI, Tesla

topics: SpaceX S-1 IPO filing and valuation
Elon Web Services / AI compute business
Nvidia Q1 earnings and market position
AI-driven workforce displacement and CEO messaging

claims.claim: SpaceX is aiming to raise $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation, which would make it the largest IPO ever by more than double Saudi Aramco's $29 billion IPO.
evidence: Largest IPO ever by more than double Aramco's $29B IPO
Expected mid-June, likely June 12, ticker SPCX
Polymarket gives 71% chance SpaceX closes first day above $2 trillion market cap

claims.claim: Anthropic is paying SpaceX $1.25 billion per month to rent Colossus One and parts of Colossus Two, totaling a $45 billion deal over three years ($15 billion/year).
evidence: $45 billion deal over three years
$15 billion per year — effectively adding a second Starlink in revenue
Contract can be canceled by either party with 90 days notice

claims.claim: SpaceX builds data centers dramatically faster than competitors and at lower cost, with construction times dropping from 122 days to 91 days to 66 days across successive facilities.
evidence: First data center: 122 days
Second: 91 days
Third: 66 days

claims.claim: Cursor's Composer 2.5 model became Pareto dominant on AI coding benchmarks after only 3-4 weeks of reinforcement learning on Colossus Two using Cursor's proprietary coding data.
evidence: Composer 2.5 is the same base model as Composer 2 (Kami k.25) with only RL applied
Cursor allegedly has more tokens of coding data than exists on the public internet
Composer 2.5 is the most selected model on Cursor

claims.claim: Nvidia reported Q1 revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year and 20% quarter-over-quarter, with $58 billion net income, $48 billion free cash flow, and 75% gross margins.
evidence: $81.6B revenue, 85% YoY growth
$58B net income, $48B free cash flow
75% gross margins, $5.3 trillion market cap

claims.claim: Nvidia's CPU business is expected to hit $20 billion this year, making it overnight one of the world's largest CPU manufacturers.
evidence: Nvidia announced $20B CPU business projection for the year
Attributed to Nvidia's unique position working with every AI lab enabling co-design

claims.claim: The AI compute market is cross-sectionally inefficient, as valuations of power/cooling/optical companies and DRAM/Nvidia cannot simultaneously be correct.
evidence: Memory makers at 3-5x PE, Nvidia at low PE
Power, cooling, optical names discounting very different futures than chip companies
If power/cooling multiples are correct, Nvidia and memory go up a lot; if Nvidia multiples are correct, everything else underperforms

claims.claim: Starlink generated $11.4 billion in revenue in 2024 on 50% growth with $4.4 billion in operating income and over 10 million subscribers.
evidence: $11.4B revenue, 50% growth
$4.4B operating income
10 million paying subscribers

quote_candidates.text: the most you can hope for here is you keep this job for some amount of time and train your way out of it
You reduce humans to a label called the measurer. And then you're like, I'm going to lay off all the measurers.
We're listening too much to the inventors of AI. I know that's appealing. They're geniuses. They're smart. We need to be listening to the frontline factory workers who are using AI
quote_candidates.speaker: Sham Sankar

quote_candidates.text: Anthropic is paying SpaceX wait for it 1.25 billion a month to rent out Colossus one and parts of Colossus two. It's a $45 billion deal over three years 15 billion a year.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jason Calacanis

quote_candidates.text: they build data centers dramatically faster than anyone else at a lower cost
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

quote_candidates.text: composer 2.5 is literally well outside the Pareto frontier. And that's after three weeks.
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin Baker

topics: Macro inflation and bond yield concerns
US-China tech diplomacy and chip sales

claims.claim: GPUs have a useful life of 10-15 years when paired with domain-specific accelerators, making long-term asset-backed financing viable
evidence: CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator said customers sign six-year contracts and pay in advance
CoreWeave's lowest financing rate is 6%
Older GPUs can be used with GROK or Cerebras accelerators for decode tasks, extending their utility

claims.claim: Michael Burry's bear case that GPU amortization periods are too long is dismissed as uninformed by market participants
evidence: CoreWeave CEO said customers are asking for assets coming off lease and expect year 7, 8, 9 useful life
Customers sign six-year contracts and pay in advance, implying confidence in longevity

claims.claim: US inflation is projected to reach 4.2–6% CPI in Q2, reversing the narrative of Fed rate cuts toward potential rate increases
evidence: Polymarket shows 99% chance May inflation comes in at 4.2% or higher
Survey of professional forecasts projects CPI hits 6%
10-year Treasury yield hit 4.6%, above Bessent's sub-4% goal

claims.claim: Global bond markets are flashing warning signs that could catalyze a credit crisis
evidence: Japan's 30-year yield at a record high of 5.1%
UK yields at highest since the Great Financial Crisis
Germany yields at highest since 2011
30-year US Treasury at 5.2%

claims.claim: Global debt-to-GDP at 310% creates a structural path toward currency collapse, inflation, and asset price inflation as governments print money to service debt
evidence: Global debt to GDP is 310%
Spending problems at federal, state, and local levels across countries sustain existing leverage
Currency collapse leads to money printing which inflates asset values

claims.claim: America is relatively advantaged by the Strait of Hormuz closure due to energy self-sufficiency and AI leadership
evidence: US is the world's largest oil and gas producer and exporter
US natural gas prices (NG1) are down this year while LNG prices are up 100-200% globally
China, Japan, India, and Europe depend on oil transiting the Strait of Hormuz

claims.claim: Selling deprecated Nvidia GPUs to China reduces the odds of China developing its own alternative chip ecosystem, which is stabilizing for US AI leadership
evidence: Alternative Chinese ecosystem would be more power-hungry
US chips bring in optical interconnects earlier for scale-up fabrics
Gavin argues this is the highest probability path to keeping America ahead in AI

claims.claim: The US-China tech CEO summit produced no grand deal, only incremental announcements including aircraft purchases, agricultural commitments, and some H100/A200 chip sales to Baidu
evidence: Some soybeans sold, some H100s and A200s sold to Baidu
No definitive policy breakthrough on tariffs or broader chip sales
Putin met with Xi immediately after the US visit, signaling continued China-Russia alignment

quote_candidates.text: Global debt to GDP is 310%. Reserve currency status to the side. The spending problem at the federal, state, local level. The spending problem at every country to basically keep economies growing to support existing leverage ultimately creates a cascading effect. It ultimately breaks.
quote_candidates.speaker: Friedberg

quote_candidates.text: The force of gravity is inevitable. The rollercoaster will roll down. We will throw our hands in the air and we will scream, whee, as we go for the ride.
quote_candidates.speaker: Friedberg

quote_candidates.text: If you can get an asset-backed loan, an asset-backed financing, for GPUs at a lower rate than other chips, that's a profound advantage.
quote_candidates.speaker: Kevin

quote_candidates.text: If they didn't think it had a six-year lifespan, they wouldn't be signing a six-year contract. Pretty straightforward. And they can't get enough of them.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jason

quote_candidates.text: I think selling deprecated Nvidia GPUs to China lowers the odds of them developing their own alternative ecosystem, which would be a lot power hungrier, because you bring in optical a lot earlier for scale-up fabrics.
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin

quote_candidates.text: Every day the Strait of Hormuz is closed is relatively good for America. It's terrible for Europe. It is terrible for Asia. Japan and China need that oil.
quote_candidates.speaker: Gavin

guest_candidates.name: Gavin Baker
guest_candidates.title: Portfolio Manager / Investor, Atreides Management (implied by managing 100+ positions with 30-person team)
